text_excerpt:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with desmoid abdominal mass- cryoablation.
Staged treatment, last treated ___  // cryoablation to abdominal wall mass

COMPARISON:  CT interventional procedure on ___.  CT abdomen and
pelvis on ___.

TECHNIQUE:  OPERATORS:  Dr. ___, attending radiologist, performed the
procedure.  Drs.  ___ fellows assisted.  Dr. ___
___ supervised the trainee during the key components of the procedure
when applicable
And reviewed and agree with the trainee's findings.
ANESTHESIA:  General anesthesia was administered by the anesthesiology
department.
MEDICATIONS:  For full details please refer to anesthesiology notes.
CONTRAST: None.
RADIATION DOSE:  3,862 mGy-cm.

PROCEDURE:  1.  Cryoablation of the residual desmoid tumor in the lower right
lower abdominal wall.

PROCEDURE DETAILS:

Following explanation of the risks, benefits and alternatives to the
procedure, written informed consent was obtained from the patient. The patient
was then brought to the computed tomography suite and placed supine on the
imaging table.  General anesthesia was induced by the anesthesiologist. 
Following scout imaging, the skin was marked and draped in the usual sterile
fashion.

Under continuous ultrasound guidance, a 3.8 mm cryoablation probe was advanced
into the residual desmoid tumor.  Subsequently a 2.4 mm cryoablation probe was
advanced parallel to the first probe within the tumor.  Subsequently, at a
more inferior location, two 3.8 mm cryoablation probes were placed in
para
